column1:
Food spots near 1 Civic/BCI, Woodbine MS, and 140 Borough; some are takeout spots, some are sit-down restaurants, and some may be just for taking some snacks into the office! 😊

Managed by Kevin :)

column2: Note

it doesn't look like this thairoomgrand location has a lunch special, and while i have not been here before, these chains are normally very consistent. menu looks inexpensive, so it is worth a go! (ETOBICOKE)
long standing chinese hakka food. big portion, inexpensive pricetags, lots of oil. not good if you're dieting but sometimes you need a plate of chili chicken (SCARBOROUGH)
newer som tum jinda location in fairview mall; other locations come very highly reviewed (NORTH YORK)

column1: شاورما حطب و نار Charc Shawarma
column2: shawarma that comes recommended by many of the west FS folk (ETOBICOKE)

fish and chips by martingrove and burnhamthorpe. supposed to be not bad! apparently they even used to serve haggis, but no longer. they've got a cod and chips + drink lunch special as well (ETOBICOKE)

well known etobicoke italian bakery. i have never ever happened to be in the area for this, so i am primed and ready to go if anyone else wants to come with me. i've been warned that as they have a massive selection of items, not everything is going to be a hit (ETOBICOKE)
greek spot at cloverdale mall, not bad actually, reminded me of when the NS team would get jimmy the greek from STC, taste was extremely similar (ETOBICOKE)

have not tried but but i have heard so many great things about this place. locally owned italian pasta made from scratch. takeout only, but looks like it would be amazing if you're in the mood for pasta. (ETOBICOKE)
cafe and other cafe-foods across cloverdale mall (ETOBICOKE)
recommended by kevin chau, he said that the group used to go from time to time for sushi, ramen, and other japanese items. (ETOBICOKE)
yet another burger spot to try :D (ETOBICOKE)
classic toronto chain, really good pizza (ETOBICOKE)
local recommended spot for vada pav, masala dosa, pav bhaji, thali, etc. (ETOBICOKE)
another local burger spot (ETOBICOKE)
OG burger spot with a special grape drink (ETOBICOKE)
donny took us with ziggy and the others to this polish place. i liked it a lot! nofrills and the schnitzel seemed fresh (ETOBICOKE)
punjabi food, looks popular! (ETOBICOKE)
typical korean fare, west side in mississauga (ETOBICOKE)
uyghur food, noodles, meat, etc. (ETOBICOKE)
lebanese style kabob and other dishes. this place looks pretty fancy, maybe better for a special occasion (ETOBICOKE)
afghani style kabob, fast-food style place, but they cook everything fresh which is always nice (ETOBICOKE)
brunch food, if you're ever in the mood for that! bacon and eggs, sandwiches, pancakes, etc. (ETOBICOKE)
yemeni food, i have never tried before but the reviews look promising! (ETOBICOKE)
another thai spot at york mills and don mills, this place has a lunch special while chiang mai (which seems to be better liked) does not. seems like good value! (NORTH YORK)

thai, short drive south of peanut plaza. this place is a little more fancy and has a brunch menu, which is a little crazy to me lol (kind of a thai-western fusion, even more so than western thai food already is) (NORTH YORK)
trinidadian food and supposedly the best doubles in toronto!! (SCARBOROUGH)
amin recommended this spot, looks like they are well known for both their kaleh pacheh in addition to the other typical persian options (NORTH YORK)
another hakka chinese spot (SCARBOROUGH)
little patty shop south of thomson memorial park  (SCARBOROUGH)
burger shop at victoria park and finch, no seating inside but probably nice to eat on a "reasonable weather" day outside (NORTH YORK)
great jamaican food and patties :) (SCARBOROUGH) (NORTH YORK)
little viet/chinese/thai place inside the peanut plaza mall, i found the pho to be ok but a little too oily for my liking. other options look good and is inexpensive (NORTH YORK)
the famous montreal shawarma in the GTA. i have not been to this location, but i have been wanting to give it a go (just not had a good opportunity) (NORTH YORK)

this place has opened up a lot of locations recently, little crispy chinese "crepes" with meat filling + other small dishes. i don't mind this sort of food but it can be a little one-dimensional (NORTH YORK)

hong kong cafe specialties like cantonese baked casseroles, tea, and other small dishes. this place opened pretty recently in fairview mall and while the reviews don't look especially promising, i think it's worth a try :) (NORTH YORK)

seems like a place you'd only come to if you had a little extra time. armenian/middle eastern food; not a lot of places serving some of these dishes, so worth a go if there is a day you're wanting a "long lunch" at (NORTH YORK)
congee, cantonese dishes, and a big menu at finch and leslie. i have not been to this location but congee places like this are always pretty consistent and good (NORTH YORK)
authorized reseller of allan's jamaican pastries located right in peanut plaza, have not tried yet but would be a very easy way to get some snacks for the team (NORTH YORK)

column1: Shi MiaoDao (Ten Seconds) Yunnan Rice Noodle (NorthYork) 十秒到云南过桥米线
column2: yunnan style rice noodles, looks like plenty of options, quick service (NORTH YORK)

i might be getting baited but i have heard this place has some really good fish and chips. i feel like every fish and chips spot i've ever tried here has just been an oily mess (and this place probably is) but on the off chance this is actually good it might be worth a go (SCARBOROUGH)
donnie-approved malay shop. takeout only, with long wait times. good to order ahead of time via phone. char kway teow was good, laksa ok. ask donnie for what he thinks is best :D (SCARBOROUGH)

fried chicken, sliders, fries, mac, etc. have not been but have been told this place is really good. probably good on a day where you know the afternoon is going to be uneventful as you'll probably need a nap later (SCARBOROUGH)
recommended spot for samosas and other sri lankan/south indian snack (SCARBOROUGH)
fancier greek place, have not tried but environment, service, and food all look quite good :) (SCARBOROUGH)
have not been here but there is an italian bakery/deli across from the roti hut. looks to be very popular. would like to give it a go (SCARBOROUGH)

little bit east of mccowan and sheppard, another west indian roti and doubles spot. i had it once and everything was very good, but i recommend against ordering bone-in (so much bone, so little meat!) (SCARBOROUGH)

apparently this was a place that the telecom team enjoyed going to when they were out east. west indian roti, doubles, etc. mona's roti is also nearby, may be worth trying both out :) i have not been yet! (SCARBOROUGH)
huge portions of greek food. amin seems to really like this place, and i thought it was pretty good too. (ETOBICOKE)

column1: Brandt Meat Manufacturing Plant & European Food Market Factory Outlet
column2: this was one of my favourite places to come when i was at civic. little european grocery/butcher that has a hot table with sausages, schnitzel, cabbage rolls, etc. probably not the best if you are more than 3-4 people but i have always enjoyed lunch + some shopping (ETOBICOKE)
ikea lunch :) they might have some discounts on some days, worth giving it a look (NORTH YORK)
andy and i gave this place in peanut plaza a try, i thought it was above average compared to other cantonese bbq spots, and was very inexpensive (NORTH YORK)

andy amin and gave this a visit as our first lunch outing as a group, i think our selections could have been a little better but congee places like this are always going to be consistent, and only gets better as the group scales!! (ETOBICOKE)
i have not been but i hear starsky is a great grocery store. unsure of how good their hot table is but i'd like to give it a go sometime (ETOBICOKE)
tiny little butcher with a hot table that serves schnitzel and a rotating weekly menu. have not tried, but the sausages they make here are supposed to be really good as well (ETOBICOKE)

column1: Tropical South by the Cuban Thing
column2: idk if this place actually exists or not but apparently its a food truck serving cuban style food. it looks really good but it might not even be a thing (ETOBICOKE)

SO FAR my favourite shawarma spot in the west. huge portion, tender meat, and a reasonable amount of rice, salad, sauce (which is what makes or breaks these sorts of restaurants for me). would recommend to get here earlier than later, as sometimes there is a bit of a line (ETOBICOKE)
family owned lebanese restaurant. shawarma, kebab, etc. would love to give this place a try sometime (ETOBICOKE)
banh mi, really inexpensive and pretty good taste, but it's a bit of a drive (ETOBICOKE)
popular toronto tacos chain that also does burritos, tortas, and quesadillas. haven't been to this location, but others have always been pretty good! (ETOBICOKE)
the etobicoke ikea. look for a discount on lunch meals, can be a fantastic deal! (ETOBICOKE)
one of two pho ngoc yen's in the area. both are roughly the same distance from the two TDSB ITS west buildings. great pho and big portions!! (ETOBICOKE)
the other pho ngoc yen location in this area. great pho!!! (ETOBICOKE)
hawker style chinese food in a little mall by sheppard and midland. crazy amount of options, but not all are great... but still amazing variety and inexpensive (SCARBOROUGH)

as a co-op student with the NS team, i frequently used to leave home early to grab breakfast (which i don't even normally eat!) from here. the breakfast combos are all great value and taste great when you get there early in the morning when they are fresh. especially love the medu vada (SCARBOROUGH)

tacos, burritos, and quesadillas. this place sounds very nice, would like to give it a try sometime. they have vegan options as well as some weird options... not my cup of tea but maybe someone else's :p (ETOBICOKE)

i took greg, sham, and andy here. this is a new korean spot, seems family owned and very homey. idk what the other korean spots in the area are like, but i found it to be a little underwhelming compared to other places in korean hotspots (not that anything tasted bad, by any means!!) (ETOBICOKE)
allwyns is a staple across the GTA, but tbh i have actually never had any patties from any location. i suggested we give this spot a go next time we want a snack for the office (ETOBICOKE)
i think i'd have to be in a very specific mood for this, but this is a spot known for chicken wings. apparently they are some of the best on this side of the city. (ETOBICOKE)
